overall_summary: ElizaOS reached significant hardware milestones by booting on an emulated RISC-V chip while simultaneously shipping v2.0.2 to standardize agent identity and cloud infrastructure. Strategic focus has pivoted exclusively toward core elizaOS development, resulting in the cancellation of planned project airdrops.
key_facts: Eliza v3 successfully booted ElizaOS Debian RISC-V 64-bit ISO on a QEMU chip emulator (eliza e1) in 35 seconds.
ElizaOS v2.0.2 was released on May 20, 2026, introducing the 'Owner+Agent' identity architecture and job-queue lifecycle management.
The Babylon airdrop for elizaOS holders has been officially cancelled due to budget constraints.
A new DeFi plugin for Eliza agents on Base and Aave was released as NPM package @centinel-risk-protocol/eliza-plugin-centinel.
Management confirmed the ElizaOS team is pivoting to a single-token utility model similar to the ETH or SOL ecosystems.
Milady app revenue buybacks were confirmed to apply specifically to the BNB version of the token.
GitHub PR #7197 integrated @elizaos/vault for cross-platform secrets encryption using OS keychains.
PR #7851 enabled OAuth support for X, Shopify, and Calendly to support distinct user and agent roles.
open_questions: Is there a physical Eliza E1 chip being developed in tandem with the emulator?
How can someone connect a Claude AI agent to AgentKey?
Is the CA on Coingecko correct for bnb/base/eth?
Are there any updates for degenai?

new_issues_prs.title: chore(release): develop → main (v2.0.2)
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Major release promoting core agent runtime updates and cloud platform fixes to main.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(vault): @elizaos/vault — cross-platform secrets vault
new_issues_prs.author: Dexploarer
new_issues_prs.significance: Implements encryption-at-rest for sensitive plugin data using OS-level keychains.

new_issues_prs.title: vulkan: Qwen2.5-14B bandwidth-efficiency cliff on Lunar Lake iGPU
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Identified critical performance degradation in 14B models on specific Intel hardware kernel paths.

overall_focus.claim: Development focus centered on 'Owner+Agent' identity modeling, migrating lifecycle tasks to job queues, and optimizing for Intel Lunar Lake/NPU hardware.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on the Eliza E1 emulated chip milestone and the strategic pivot away from the Babylon airdrop to focus on elizaOS as a primary eth/sol-style utility token.
Announcements of new DeFi plugins and significant documentation requests for v2.0.0 features. Developers are actively coordinating on trading bot integrations.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community members expressed strong support for the core team focusing on a single-token utility model rather than diluting focus with external airdrops.
Users requested clearer release notes for v2.0.0 and inquired about the legal relationship between Milady and elizaOS.

strategic_insights.insight: The successful emulation of the eliza e1 chip on RISC-V suggests an intent to move toward sovereign hardware, potentially reducing dependency on standard GPU/CPU providers.
implications_or_questions: Does the project have the capital for physical chip fabrication if emulation tests continue to succeed?

strategic_insights.insight: Cancelling the Babylon airdrop signals a shift toward fiscal conservatism and token scarcity, prioritizing the elizaOS token's internal utility over ecosystem expansion through incentives.
implications_or_questions: Will the market provide the necessary liquidity for ecosystem tokens if the core team remains hands-off?

market_analysis.observation: BYTE Protocol reported over 10,000 messages settled on testnet, signaling developer interest in data settlement layers on Arbitrum.
market_analysis.relevance: Potential integration target for elizaOS agents requiring verifiable on-chain data settlement.
